note: Example-only Superset security remediation issue templates. Use these for demo mode or as a formatting guide. For an actual take-home submission, run Bandit, pip-audit, Safety, or a focused manual review against your Superset fork and curate real findings into demo/findings.json. Do not claim CVEs unless scanner output explicitly provides them.

findings.title: Scanner-derived finding: dependency package requires security review and narrow upgrade
findings.risk: A dependency scanner reported that a package version used by the Superset fork has a security advisory. The risk and upgrade target must be confirmed from the scanner output before filing the real issue.
acceptance_criteria: Confirm the affected package, installed version, and patched version from scanner output.
Apply the narrowest safe version bump or constraint update.
Verify dependency resolution still succeeds.
suggested_verification: Re-run pip-audit or Safety after the dependency change.
Run a focused import or test path that exercises the affected dependency if one is identifiable.
non_goals: Do not claim a CVE unless the scanner output explicitly names one.

findings.title: Security hygiene finding: add input validation for user-controlled identifier
findings.risk: A user-controlled identifier appears to flow into security-sensitive lookup or filtering logic without an explicit allowlist, schema check, or normalization step. The real issue should reference the confirmed source and sink.
acceptance_criteria: Add explicit validation or normalization for the affected input.
Reject malformed values with a safe error response.
Add focused tests for accepted, rejected, and boundary inputs.
suggested_verification: Run the targeted tests for the affected handler or service.
Manually exercise one valid and one invalid request path.
non_goals: Do not redesign the full request handling flow.
Do not introduce broad validation changes outside the confirmed path.

findings.title: Scanner-derived finding: review path handling for filesystem access
findings.category: insecure temp file or path handling
findings.risk: A scanner flagged a path or temporary-file handling pattern that may allow unsafe path construction, weak file permissions, or unreliable cleanup. The real issue should cite the exact scanner finding and affected call site.
acceptance_criteria: Confirm whether the flagged path is user-influenced or security-sensitive.
Use safe path joining, normalization, or tempfile APIs as appropriate.
Ensure cleanup and permission behavior are explicit.
Add regression coverage for the confirmed risky path.
suggested_verification: Re-run the scanner rule against the affected file.
Run focused tests for the file or export/import workflow.
non_goals: Do not alter unrelated filesystem workflows.

findings.title: Scanner-derived finding: hardcoded secret-like value in test fixture
findings.risk: A scanner identified a secret-like value in a test fixture. The value may be intentionally fake, but replacing it with an obvious placeholder can reduce noise and avoid normalizing realistic secrets in tests.
acceptance_criteria: Replace the secret-like value with an obviously fake placeholder or generated value.
Keep the test behavior unchanged.
Avoid global scanner suppressions.
suggested_verification: Re-run the scanner rule that flagged the fixture.
non_goals: Do not rotate credentials unless a real secret is confirmed.
Do not suppress the scanner for the whole repository.

findings.title: Security hygiene finding: make failure handling explicit in security-sensitive check
findings.risk: A security-relevant check may return an ambiguous result when an internal dependency fails, times out, or returns unexpected data. The remediation should preserve behavior while making failure states explicit and observable.
acceptance_criteria: Return a safe failure state when the check cannot complete.
Log actionable context without exposing sensitive values.
Add tests for dependency failure, malformed response, or timeout behavior.
suggested_verification: Run focused tests for the affected validation or authorization check.
non_goals: Do not add new external services.
Do not change unrelated authorization behavior.

findings.title: Security hygiene finding: restrict debug behavior to explicit local development mode
findings.source: manual security review or Semgrep output
findings.risk: A debug-only behavior appears reachable without a sufficiently explicit local-development guard. In a real issue, cite the exact configuration pathway and why it could be enabled outside local development.
acceptance_criteria: Require an explicit local-development condition before enabling debug behavior.
Preserve legitimate local developer ergonomics.
Add tests covering local and non-local configuration cases.
suggested_verification: Run configuration tests for the affected module.
Confirm production-like settings do not enable debug behavior.
non_goals: Do not remove legitimate local-only debugging support.
Do not change unrelated configuration defaults.

findings.title: Scanner-derived finding: narrow broad exception handling around sensitive logic
findings.source: Bandit, Semgrep, or manual security review
findings.risk: A broad exception handler may hide validation, authorization, or data-protection failures and allow execution to continue in a less safe state. The real issue should identify the expected exception types and the safe fallback behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Catch only expected exception types where practical.
Fail closed or return a safe error for unexpected failures.
Add focused tests for expected and unexpected failure paths.
suggested_verification: Run targeted tests for the affected module.
Manually confirm unexpected exceptions are not silently swallowed.
non_goals: Do not rewrite unrelated error handling.
Do not expose sensitive exception details to end users.

findings.title: Security hygiene finding: add regression test for previously fixed security boundary
findings.risk: A security-relevant boundary appears to rely on behavior that is not covered by a focused regression test. The remediation should add coverage for the confirmed boundary without changing production behavior unless a bug is found.
acceptance_criteria: Identify the security boundary that lacks regression coverage.
Add a focused test that fails if the boundary regresses.
Avoid broad or slow test additions.
suggested_verification: Run the new focused regression test.
Run the nearest existing test file or test class.
non_goals: Do not refactor production code unless the missing test exposes a real defect.
Do not add broad end-to-end coverage for a narrow boundary.
